practice_boundaries.detail: Skill explicitly refuses to certify claim appropriateness when study design or validation context is unclear

code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 4 Academic Writing

functional_suitability.note: Description is brief and functional but does not convey the breadth of calibration scenarios or use natural trigger language

reliability.note: Clarification-first rule is well designed; one gap: no defined behavior when study design is known but validation status is unstated

human_usability.note: Description and sample triggers use natural author-facing language that matches real manuscript revision scenarios

agent_specific.note: Good trigger phrases in sample triggers; description slightly under-specified for distinguishing from generic grammar checkers

inputs.label: Review abstract with causal overclaiming in a retrospective cohort study

assertions.text: Each overclaim mapped to its correct evidence level (association vs causation)
Issue severity classified into major/moderate/minor
Proposed rewrite maintains informative content while reducing overclaim
No stronger support invented beyond what the study provides
Calibration logic explained with reviewer-risk rationale

inputs.label: Discussion section with mechanism inflation in an omics association study

assertions.text: Supportive biology correctly distinguished from mechanism established
Validation inflation identified (external validation implied from internal only)
Section-level scope (paragraph vs sentence) determined before review
Proposed rewrites preserve scientific meaning while calibrating strength
No fabricated study precedents used to justify calibration decisions

inputs.label: Translational statement review: prediction performance claimed as clinical utility

assertions.text: Prediction vs clinical utility distinction clearly applied
Translational overreach identified and flagged as major risk
Rewrite recommendation narrows claim without voiding its scientific content
Correction priority explains why translational wording creates credibility risk
No clinical recommendation made beyond scope

inputs.label: Only title and abstract provided without study design or methods
inputs.note: Clarification-first rule triggered; partial review only with explicit missing-context statement

assertions.text: Clarification-first rule applied — missing study design context flagged before full review
Partial calibration review limited to what is observable from title/abstract alone
Missing information explicitly listed with recommended upload items
No confident calibration judgments made without study design knowledge
Severity classifications labeled 'uncertain due to missing context' where appropriate
assertions.note: Some severity labels given without sufficient qualification that they may change with full study context

inputs.label: Full manuscript pre-submission review with multiple overclaim patterns across all sections

assertions.text: All six overclaim pattern types (causal, mechanism, validation, translation, vague, evidence-level mismatch) checked
Major, moderate, and minor issues separated in output
Correction priority plan given for all major issues
No over-softening of appropriately strong claims
Explanation of calibration logic present for all major findings
assertions.note: Two moderate-severity items in stress test lacked calibration logic explanation; only correction action was listed

key_strengths: Clarification-first rule prevents confident review when study design context is insufficient — a critical safeguard
Seven distinct overclaim pattern categories (causal inflation, mechanism inflation, validation inflation, etc.) give precise diagnostic coverage
Severity classification mandate (major/moderate/minor) prevents flat stylistic reviews
Hard rules explicitly prohibit both over-softening and under-flagging — preserving calibration balance

recommendations.title: Severity labels applied with insufficient qualification in partial-context reviews
recommendations.problem: When only title/abstract is available, some severity labels were assigned without explicitly noting they may change once full study design is known.
recommendations.root_cause: The partial-review path from the clarification-first rule does not include an instruction to mark severity as 'provisional pending full context'.
recommendations.fix: Add a rule to the Clarification-First reference: any severity classification made without full study design access must be labeled 'provisional' with a note that the rating may upgrade or downgrade once complete manuscript is available.

recommendations.title: Calibration logic explanation omitted for moderate-severity items in full-manuscript review
recommendations.problem: Section G (Calibration Logic Explanation) is required for major issues but in full-manuscript stress tests, moderate-severity items sometimes only received correction actions without the 'why it matters' explanation.
recommendations.root_cause: Step 8 instructions say 'for major issues explicitly explain'; this implicitly permits skipping explanation for moderate items.
recommendations.fix: Update Step 8 to require at least one-sentence calibration logic for all moderate issues, not just major ones, since moderate items are common in real manuscripts.

recommendations.title: Description does not distinguish this skill from generic grammar or style checkers
recommendations.problem: The brief description could be confused with a general proofreader; users wanting claim-level scientific calibration may not trigger this skill over generic writing tools.
recommendations.root_cause: Description does not include domain-specific trigger language like 'overclaiming', 'causation vs association', 'translational overreach', or 'reviewer criticism risk'.
recommendations.fix: Expand description to include these key scientific concepts to improve differentiation from non-specialist writing tools.
